title: World Diving Calendar - Period Rankings
description: Destinations ranked for each of the 24 bi-monthly periods. Higher score = better diving conditions plus more headline marine life that period.

scoring.marine_excitement_bonus: sum of matched headline-species weights, capped at 25

ranked.name: Fuvahmulah
ranked.highlight: Resident tiger sharks daily; scalloped hammerheads still schooling and silvertips gathering; whale sharks possible along the drop-offs.

ranked.name: Maldives
ranked.highlight: Hammerheads Rasdhoo (dawn); Fuvahmulah tiger sharks; grey reef sharks

ranked.name: Tofo
ranked.highlight: Whale sharks, resident mantas, reef sharks

ranked.name: Jupiter
ranked.highlight: Lemon shark aggregation peaks, great hammerheads, bull sharks

ranked.name: Raja Ampat
ranked.highlight: Manta peak (Manta Sandy/Ridge); wobbegongs, walking sharks, pygmy seahorses

ranked.name: Yap
ranked.highlight: Peak manta season: courting trains of resident reef mantas stack up at Mi'l Channel cleaning stations.

ranked.name: Truk (Chuuk) Lagoon
ranked.highlight: Coral-encrusted wrecks (266+ fish sp); reef sharks, turtles, rays

ranked.name: Malpelo Island
ranked.highlight: Huge scalloped hammerhead schools; smalltooth sand tiger rises to divable depth; Galapagos sharks

ranked.name: Cocos Island
ranked.highlight: Tiger sharks; mobula schools; silky sharks; hammerheads (steady)

ranked.name: Palau
ranked.highlight: Moorish idol aggregation (full moon); mantas; grey reef sharks; whale sharks

ranked.name: Komodo National Park
ranked.highlight: South mantas PEAK (Manta Alley); hammerheads (south); macro explosion

ranked.name: Galapagos Islands
ranked.highlight: Mantas; hammerhead schools (smaller); sea lions, iguanas

ranked.name: Red Sea (Egypt)
ranked.highlight: Oceanic whitetip (south), threshers possible; reef & wreck focus

ranked.name: Protea Banks
ranked.highlight: Bull (Zambezi) sharks patrol the Southern Pinnacles; tail end of the scalloped hammerhead season with tiger sharks possible; game fish plentiful.

ranked.name: Seychelles
ranked.highlight: Hawksbill turtle nesting peak; eagle rays; whale sharks may linger

ranked.name: Sipadan Island
ranked.highlight: Barracuda tornado, jackfish, reef sharks, turtles; CNY window

ranked.name: Heron Island
ranked.region: Coral Sea / Southern Great Barrier Reef

ranked.name: Great Barrier Reef
ranked.highlight: Green turtle nesting (Raine Is); reef life

ranked.name: Cenderawasih Bay
ranked.highlight: Whale sharks feed under the Kwatisore bagans year-round; wrecks and endemic reef fish active

ranked.name: Silfra Fissure
ranked.highlight: Crystal viz, no marine life - geology dive

ranked.name: Nusa Penida
ranked.highlight: Reef mantas active at Manta Point on rich wet-season nutrients; turtles and reef fish

ranked.name: Kaş
ranked.highlight: Reef fish and grouper present but subdued; fewer pelagics in the cold water.

ranked.name: Larnaca
ranked.highlight: Quiet cool-water months; grouper, moray and bream hold on the wrecks while pelagic activity thins.

ranked.name: Chania (Crete)
ranked.highlight: Quiet cold-season reefs; resident grouper, moray and octopus still about

ranked.name: Ustica
ranked.highlight: Groupers and resident reef fish remain, but activity is subdued in the cold season and most operators are wound down for winter.

ranked.name: Vis Island
ranked.highlight: Sparse and slow in cold water; grouper and conger hold in cover, life is quiet.
